description: Colombian drug cartel that operated in the northern Valle del Cauca department, rising to prominence in the 1990s after the fragmentation of the Cali and Medellín cartels. It was led by the Calle Serna brothers until its dismantling in 2008 by Colombian and Venezuelan authorities with DEA cooperation. The cartel engaged in drug trafficking, murder, money laundering, gun-running, and human trafficking.

country_links.evidence_quote: Ramirez was arrested in Brazil on August 7, 2007. Ramirez was extradited by Brazil to the United States on Friday, August 22, 2008.
country_links.context: A cartel leader was arrested in Brazil and later extradited to the United States.

country_links.evidence_quote: Luis Hernando Gómez Bustamante ... was arrested on July 2, 2004, trying to enter Cuba on a false passport.
country_links.context: A cartel leader was arrested trying to enter Cuba, indicating the cartel had a presence or attempted presence there.

country_links.evidence_quote: Ramón Quintero Sanclemente ... was arrested in Quito (Ecuador) and immediately deported to Colombia.
country_links.context: Ramón Quintero Sanclemente, a high-ranking cartel member, was arrested in Quito, Ecuador, showing the cartel's footprint there.

country_links.evidence_quote: the Norte del Valle cartel exported more than 1.2 million pounds – or 500 metric tons – of cocaine worth in excess of $10 billion from Colombia to Mexico and ultimately to the United States for resale.
country_links.context: The cartel routed cocaine through Mexico en route to the United States.

country_links.evidence_quote: Arcángel was arrested on January 10, 2004, in Panama and extradited to New York.
country_links.context: A cartel leader was arrested in Panama and extradited to New York.

country_links.evidence_quote: Orlando Sabogal Zuluaga ... was captured ... on October 31, 2006, in a shopping mall in Spain.
country_links.context: A cartel member was captured in Spain, indicating the cartel's operations extended there.

country_links.evidence_quote: the Norte del Valle cartel exported more than 1.2 million pounds – or 500 metric tons – of cocaine worth in excess of $10 billion from Colombia to Mexico and ultimately to the United States for resale.
country_links.context: The cartel exported cocaine to the United States for resale.

country_links.evidence_quote: until its takedown in 2008 by the authorities of Colombia and Venezuela, with cooperation of the United States DEA.
country_links.context: The cartel was taken down by authorities of Colombia and Venezuela, indicating presence in Venezuela.
